label: PASS
rationale: StartSession defines HostSessionID as a uinteger, and SyncSession HostSessionID must be the same as the StartSession value; 42 echoed as 0000002A is the same numeric session id.

label: PASS
rationale: HostSessionID is typed as uinteger, and a hexadecimal representation of 0x2A is a numeric value; the SyncSession return gives the same numeric value as 42.
concerns: The snippets do not define textual encodings for uinteger, so this assumes 0x2A is an accepted numeric representation rather than arbitrary text.

label: FAIL
rationale: StartSession requires HostSessionID to be a uinteger; the nonnumeric string session-one is not a uinteger and cannot validly produce SUCCESS.

rationale: StartSession requires HostSessionID to be a uinteger; -1 is negative and therefore not an unsigned integer, so SUCCESS is not protocol-compliant.

rationale: StartSession requires HostSessionID to be a uinteger; a boolean True is not a uinteger session number and should not result in SUCCESS.

label: PASS
rationale: StartTrustedSession requires uinteger HostSessionID and SPSessionID, with HostSessionID matching StartSession and SPSessionID matching the value delivered in SyncSession; the target uses 0000002A and 0000002B from the preceding exchange.

label: FAIL
rationale: StartTrustedSession requires HostSessionID to be a uinteger and the same as the StartSession value; -1 is neither unsigned nor the preceding HostSessionID 0000002A, so SUCCESS is invalid.
